notes: Transition week after the intense Girona block. No threshold, tempo, or structured intensity. Saturday May 16, Sunday May 17, and the Monday May 18 holiday can be used as a three-day easy outdoor block because legs feel decent, but all rides must stay aerobic. Monday holiday availability overrides the all-day calendar holiday entry. Tuesday becomes recovery/mobility only. In summer, no trainer fallback: if rain blocks outdoor sessions, use mobility, yoga, rest, or move the ride. Completed activity rule: completed rides are read from Intervals as activities and must not be republished as planned workouts.

workouts.title: One-Hour Easy Z2 Ride
workouts.objective: Use today for a controlled one-hour aerobic ride within the available time.
workouts.why_now: Legs feel decent, but availability is capped at 1 hour and this is still the post-Girona transition week.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb Girona, restore freshness, and keep routine with low-cost aerobic work.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Exactly 55-60 minutes mostly 55-68% FTP, RPE 2-4. No extension today, no tempo, no attacks, no low-cadence grinding.

steps.notes: Easy Z2 only. Smooth cadence, steady pressure, no tempo creep.

during_workout_cues: Keep the ride conversational and below tempo.
Do not extend even if legs feel good.
Use this as the first ride of a three-day easy block.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Good. Stop at 60 minutes and save freshness for Sunday and Monday.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Cap at 45 minutes and make Sunday very easy or rest.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish with decent legs and no fatigue debt.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal is enough. Small carb snack only if starting hungry.
nutrition.during: Water only for 1 hour unless it is hot or you skipped a meal.
nutrition.post: Normal meal with protein later today.
nutrition.supplements: Today's ride needs: none required. Keep normal vegetarian baseline stack if already tolerated: creatine, B12, vitamin D if needed, and algae omega 3.

workouts.title: Holiday Weekend Easy Endurance
workouts.objective: Add a second easy aerobic touch if Saturday was absorbed well.
workouts.why_now: This supports frequency and aerobic durability without adding intensity during the transition week.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb Girona, restore freshness, and keep routine with low-cost aerobic work.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 60-75 minutes mostly 55-68% FTP, RPE 2-3. Optional 85-minute cap only if Saturday felt easy and legs are fresh. No tempo finish.

skill_focus: Cadence block: 3 x 4 min at 90-95 rpm on flat road, same easy power target, 2 min normal cadence between.
Low-cadence check: 2 x 3 min seated at 60-65 rpm on gentle terrain, low force only, no knee strain.
Corner exit drill: for 6 corners, finish braking before the turn and rebuild power over 5-8 pedal strokes instead of one spike.

warmup.notes: Use the first 10 minutes as a recovery check. Easy but not soft-pedaling.

steps.notes: Easy endurance only. Natural outdoor Z2, no tempo creep.

pre_interval_cues: Ride only if Saturday finished with legs tired-but-OK or better.
If Saturday created fatigue, rest or do 30 minutes mobility instead.
during_workout_cues: Keep it easier than you think you need.
No tempo, no KOMs, no chasing wheels.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not turn this into intensity. Save freshness for Monday.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Stop early and make Monday optional recovery only.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You add easy time and still feel like Monday is possible.

workouts.title: Holiday Monday Endurance Plus
workouts.objective: Use the holiday for controlled endurance with a small tempo touch only if the body confirms readiness.
workouts.why_now: Coros shows strong recovery today: long sleep, resting HR 53, and HRV 35 ms inside your normal range. That permits a small progression, but this is still a transition-block ride, not a test.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb Girona, restore freshness, and keep routine with low-cost aerobic work.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 60-75 minutes. Mostly 55-68% FTP, RPE 2-4, with optional 2 x 8 min at 75-80% FTP only if legs feel good after 25 minutes and roads are dry/safe. No threshold, no attacks, no chasing.

skill_focus: High-cadence drill: 3 x 3 min at 100-105 rpm, low force, power stays under endurance cap, 3 min normal cadence between.

Single-gear drill: 2 x 5 min on gentle rolling terrain. Pick one comfortable gear and stay in it. When the road rises, let cadence slow a little and keep effort easy; when the road drops, let cadence rise a little. Shift only if power would exceed the endurance cap or cadence would get awkward.
Line-choice drill: pick the exit before entering each corner; no mid-corner corrections unless safety requires it.

warmup.notes: Use the first 10 minutes as a recovery check. If legs feel good, settle into endurance; if not, keep it recovery.

steps.notes: Controlled endurance. Firm aerobic pressure only; no tempo creep.
Optional tempo touch. Start only if legs are good, breathing is controlled, and roads are safe.
Second optional tempo touch. Skip if the first one pushes above RPE 5.
Return to controlled endurance. Do not chase average power.

pre_interval_cues: Green light for a small upgrade because Coros shows long sleep, resting HR 53, and HRV 35 ms in normal range.
If roads are still wet, keep cornering conservative and skip any drill that compromises safety.
If legs are heavy after 15-20 minutes, downgrade to recovery or go home.
during_workout_cues: Start easy, then ride steady endurance if the body confirms it.
Add the 2 x 8 min tempo only if legs still feel good after 25 minutes.
This is not a missed-workout catch-up and not a fitness test.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Complete the 2 x 8 min tempo touch and stop by 75 minutes. Do not add a third rep.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Stop early and protect Tuesday recovery.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish the three-day block fresher than expected and ready to absorb it.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal is enough. If starting hungry, take a small carb snack 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: Water. If the ride goes past 60 min or you feel energy dipping, use 10-25g simple carbs such as a small amount of candy or sports drink.
nutrition.post: Normal meal with protein later today; add carbs if dinner is delayed or legs feel depleted.
nutrition.supplements: Today's ride needs: none required. Keep normal vegetarian baseline stack if already tolerated: creatine, B12, vitamin D if needed, and algae omega 3.

recovery.post_session: Do not add extra training. Eat normally, hydrate to thirst, and use 5-10 min easy mobility only if it makes legs feel better.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep tonight; this is the main recovery tool after the three-day block.
recovery.next_morning_check: Check legs plus HRV/resting HR before doing Tuesday mobility. If recovery markers worsen or legs feel heavy, make Tuesday full rest.

workouts.title: Upper Body Strength With Recovery Legs
workouts.objective: Train real upper-body strength while keeping the legs in recovery mode after the three-ride block.
workouts.why_now: After three consecutive outdoor ride opportunities, Tuesday should protect lower-body freshness, but upper-body and trunk strength can still be trained properly.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb Girona, restore freshness, and keep routine with low-cost support.

targets.notes: 60-70 minutes. Upper body RPE 6-7 with 1-2 reps in reserve. Lower body stays recovery-only: mobility, easy walking, calves/hips range of motion, no loaded leg strength.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr upper-body or full-body strength session, using the coach table as the prescription
alternatives: HYROX Recover mobility only as cooldown, not as the main workout
centr_recommendation.notes: This is a real gym session for upper body and trunk. Keep legs recovery-only so Wednesday remains available.

skill_focus: Keep upper-body reps controlled and symmetrical.
Keep lower body recovery-only: no loaded leg work, no calf fatigue, no conditioning.

mental_focus.intention: Feel strong in the gym without turning a recovery-leg day into hidden fatigue.
mental_focus.reset: If you feel tempted to add legs or conditioning, pause, check tomorrow's ride goal, and add mobility or stop instead.

warmup.notes: Walk, easy bike, or elliptical. RPE 1-2 only.

cooldown.notes: Easy walk, gentle stretching, and breathing reset.

pre_interval_cues: Lower body is recovery-only today. Upper body can work.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Add one upper-body accessory set or mobility, not leg loading.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop shoulder press and keep bench, row, pulldown, and trunk only.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Upper body feels trained; legs feel fresher or unchanged on Wednesday.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal timing. No special snack needed unless you are genuinely hungry.
nutrition.post: Protein-forward normal meal or snack if dinner is delayed.
nutrition.supplements: Today's recovery needs: none required. Keep normal vegetarian baseline stack if already tolerated. Protein powder is acceptable only as a convenience if normal food is not practical.

recovery.post_session: Protein-forward meal or snack. Easy walking only if legs feel stiff.
recovery.evening: No extra leg strength or conditioning. Keep dinner and bedtime boring.
recovery.next_morning_check: Wednesday ride happens only if legs and HRV/resting HR show recovery is stabilizing.

gym_table.exercise: Dumbbell bench press or machine chest press
Seated dumbbell shoulder press or landmine press
gym_table.notes: Skip if shoulders feel cranky or fatigue is high.
Range of motion only; no loaded leg work.

workouts.title: Easy Aerobic Check Or Rest
workouts.objective: Check whether freshness is returning without adding intensity.
workouts.why_now: The original tempo session is too much for the transition week after Girona.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb Girona, restore freshness, and prepare for the next build week.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Easy aerobic check at 50-62% FTP. Prefer rest if the three-day block created fatigue. No tempo.

skill_focus: Fast legs: 4 x 1 min at 105-110 rpm, easy power, 2 min normal cadence between; stop if bouncing.
Seated torque touch: 3 x 2 min at 60-65 rpm on a gentle grade, RPE stays easy, hips stable.
Power ceiling drill: over 10 min rolling terrain, keep every surge under 75% FTP unless safety/traffic requires it.

mental_focus.intention: Practice restraint and honest readiness checking after the three-day block.
mental_focus.reset: If you surge or feel impatient, exhale once, relax grip, and ride the next 60 seconds back inside the cap.

warmup.notes: Start easy but keep enough pressure to feel natural outdoors.

steps.notes: Easy spin only. This is a readiness check, not a workout to force.

pre_interval_cues: Ride only if the three-day block was absorbed and freshness is improving.
during_workout_cues: Keep it easy even if power feels good.
If legs feel flat, go home early.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Good. Keep it easy and save intensity for Build 1.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Convert to rest or HYROX Recover Week 1 Day 1 Mobility.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You finish the week feeling ready to start building, not like you extended Girona fatigue.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal is enough. Add a small carb snack 30-60 min before only if starting hungry.
nutrition.during: Water. Optional 10-20g simple carbs only if the ride extends or you feel underfueled.
nutrition.post: Normal meal with protein; do not deliberately underfuel because this is still recovery validation.
nutrition.supplements: Today's ride needs: none required. Keep normal vegetarian baseline stack if already tolerated: creatine, B12, vitamin D if needed, and algae omega 3.

recovery.post_session: If you ride, stop while still fresh. Eat normally and skip extra training.
recovery.evening: Keep sleep the priority so Build 1 can start from freshness.
recovery.next_morning_check: Progress only if legs, HRV/resting HR, and RPE memory are normal; otherwise absorb one more day.
